checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013, prior year comparative cash flow/income statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or inventory-related concept is present in the provided financial series; company reports CostOfServices/GrossProfit consistent with a services business.
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 income statement line items provided

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=276330954.86 (2014-01-01/2014-12-31) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=510500608.32 (2014-01-01/2014-12-31) = 54.1% margin, consistent with GrossProfit=249719456.44 (2013) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=453889907.19 (2013) = 55.0%

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: OperatingIncomeLoss=2253664.28 (2015-01-01/2015-03-31) swinging to OperatingIncomeLoss=-8611015.74 (2015-07-01/2015-09-30) despite rising SalesRevenueServicesNet=135923826.29 (2015-07-01/2015-09-30)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31) then AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=245067.97 (2011-06-30) and unchanged through (2012-09-30), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34345555.24 (2010-12-31) to 79822482.12 (2012-09-30)
evidence.location: 10-Q Q3 2012 receivables note, comparative allowance trend

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filings occurred on a consistent ~75-90 day post-fiscal-year-end cadence (2012-03-29, 2013-03-18, 2014-03-17, 2015-03-16) with no NT 10-K/NT 10-Q filings present in the chronology.

10-K/A filing_date 2012-04-26 shortly after 10-K filing_date 2012-03-29, and 8-K/A filings on 2012-03-05, 2012-10-24, and 2015-08-14 coincide with acquisition-related goodwill jumps (e.g., Goodwill=158285077.26 at 2012-09-30), consistent with routine pro forma/Part III amendments rather than restatements.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual balance sheet, income statement and partial cash flow data are provided from 2009 through Q3 2015 across ~19-25 data points per concept, covering pre- and post-IPO periods.

NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations is reported mostly at annual and Q1 frequency only (e.g., only Q1 quarterly values given for 2012-2015), limiting full quarterly cash-flow trend analysis.

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extension of customer payment terms (e.g., channel stuffing, bill-and-hold, or aggressive multi-element allocation) causing receivables to build up faster than sales; implied DSO rose from roughly 75-80 days in 2012-2013 to ~97-100 days by mid/late 2015.

Under-provisioning of bad debt reserve during a period of rapid receivables growth (2011-2012), which would understate bad debt expense and overstate net receivables and net income during that window; ratio of allowance to gross receivables fell from ~1.7% (2010) to ~0.4% (2011-2012) even as receivables nearly doubled.

Large and persistent non-cash add-backs (D&A, likely stock-based compensation, deferred items) cause operating cash flow to substantially and consistently exceed reported net income, including in loss years, indicating earnings quality reliant on non-cash reconciling items rather than core profitability trends.

top_signals: Days Sales Outstanding trended up from ~75-80 days (2012-2013) to ~97-100 days (2015), with AccountsReceivableNetCurrent growth persistently outpacing SalesRevenueServicesNet growth in recent quarters

Allowance for doubtful accounts fell sharply relative to receivables during 2011-2012 high-growth period (ratio ~1.7% to ~0.4%) before later recovering, suggesting a period of potential under-reserving
Persistent, large divergence between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow across multiple years (e.g., 2011 and 2014), indicating heavy reliance on non-cash reconciling items

Sudden swing to material net losses and operating losses in Q2-Q3 2015 despite continued revenue growth, coinciding with the period of highest DSO, raising questions about expense timing/reserve adequacy relative to revenue recognition
